alerts.title: US-Israeli strike on Iran's Larak Island kills three in Strait of Hormuz
alerts.summary: Multiple outlets (Straits Times, Crypto Briefing, Anadolu) report a joint US-Israeli strike on Larak Island and Iranian vessels at the Strait of Hormuz, with three reported killed. Larak hosts IRGC Navy missile-boat and submarine infrastructure ~26.5°N, 56.2°E — striking it materially escalates beyond the southern Iran 'self-defense' box and puts the world's most critical oil chokepoint at direct risk. Brent crude is now at $96.14.

alerts.title: Multiple US MQ-9 Reaper drones reportedly downed near Bandar Abbas
alerts.summary: Middle East Spectator (~38K views) reports multiple American drones, likely MQ-9 Reapers, were shot down near Bandar Abbas while attempting to locate Iranian missile launch sites targeting US warships. If confirmed, this represents the first significant US airframe losses of the current exchange and signals improved Iranian air-defense targeting against ISR assets supporting CENTCOM's strike campaign.

alerts.title: Israeli strike on eastern Lebanon village kills 12; IDF calls up more troops
alerts.summary: WKRG and Middle East Eye report an Israeli strike on a village in eastern Lebanon killed 12, with additional IDF strikes hitting Kafr Sir in the south as Netanyahu vows to intensify operations. Israel has called up further reserves for the Lebanon front, expanding the second-front escalation Tehran warned would 'collapse' the Qatar talks.

alerts.title: Trump issues ultimatum: Iran's enriched uranium must be surrendered or destroyed in place
alerts.summary: On Truth Social (relayed via ME Spectator, ~43K views), President Trump demanded Iran's 60% enriched uranium stockpile be 'immediately turned over' to the US for destruction, or destroyed in place under IAEA-equivalent supervision. The ultimatum directly contradicts Reuters reporting that Khamenei insists enriched material must remain in Iran, and lands with breakout time still estimated at 1–2 weeks.

alerts.title: Iran-linked hackers send direct threat messages to US service members
alerts.summary: Stars and Stripes reports US troops are receiving threatening messages from an Iran-linked hacker group, coinciding with documented GPS jamming across the Middle East and CENTCOM deploying a defensive 'umbrella' of EW/tech assets to shield shipping in the Strait of Hormuz (DefenseScoop). The personalized targeting of individual servicemembers marks a shift from broad infrastructure attacks toward direct psychological-cyber pressure on deployed forces.

analysis_note: US-Iran kinetic exchange has expanded into the Strait of Hormuz proper with the Larak Island strike and reported MQ-9 losses, even as Trump issues a public nuclear ultimatum and Israel escalates a parallel Lebanon front — threat level remains at 9/10 with diplomacy and combat now running in tandem.
